methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals institutional commitment to distributed AI literacy beyond elite tech workers, creating potential peer networks that could democratize capability across Richmond's diverse workforce sectors.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents bottom-up capability building in an economically transitioning region, testing whether deep AI fluency can take root outside traditional tech corridors through localized peer networks.

signals.title: NVTC Spotlights Region's AI Trailblazers: Meet the 2026 AI50 - PR Newswire
signals.signal_reading: NVTC's AI50 recognition program concentrates visibility and validation around Northern Virginia's existing AI trailblazers, reinforcing the region's deep expertise cluster while offering no mechanism for knowledge transfer to other Virginia regions.

signals.title: NoVA School Systems Grapple with AI’s Role in the Classroom - Northern Virginia Magazine
signals.signal_reading: NoVA school systems grappling with AI in classrooms suggests platform-mediated adoption by educators—shallow integration driven by commercial tools rather than deep pedagogical transformation or student AI literacy development.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads MLS deploying AI personalization tools exemplifies shallow, distributed adoption—real estate agents become platform users rather than AI-capable professionals, spreading access without building transferable technical depth.
